observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
make a communication to the province about the situation of the funds lent to the people as it cannot be collected and request for write off, if warranted; and,
request for write-off of the Due from GOCCs and Due from LGU’s accounts which were considered dormant and identities of the same could no longer be determined.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income with the Schedule of Delinquent Taxpayers prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position of the Municipality are fairly presented.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor, Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on the land owned by the LGU.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations as follows:
The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Accountant.
The General Services Officer:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map;
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management conduct physical count and inspection of unserviceable properties so that they may be properly disposed of and dropped from the books of accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management:
direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS; and,
direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide appropriation for the premiums of such insurance coverage with the GSIS.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the balance of the Breeding Stocks account to properly reflect the status of the said account in its Financial Statements.

We recommended that management strictly comply with DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, by taking into consideration the following:
Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the PPA, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; and,
Adopt strategies to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the social, economic and environmental outcomes essential in the attainment of the constitutional objectives of a desired quality of life for all.

We recommended the following:
Ensure that the MPDC regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time;
Conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20 percent DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality, otherwise, reprogram the identified PPAs to priority PPAs; and,
Realign or reprogram the unutilized/unimplemented PPAs identified to Priority Development PPAs in accordance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point personnel continue the gathering of data for its GAD database containing the sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics in order to be able to come up with realistic and focused program, projects and activities that are gender-responsive and gender related and incorporate these in the GAD Plan and Budget for costing.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant revert the CY 2015 and CY 2016 balances back to General Fund the same be appropriated for other social services; and
The MDRRMO prepare an updated LDRRMFIP to incorporate all of the previous years’ unutilized PPAs that are for implementation.

We recommended that management comply with the requirements on the submission of the perfected contracts to the Auditor within five working days pursuant to the afore-cited regulations.
Likewise, submit the documents enumerated in Items 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management limit the pakyaw labor contracts to ₱0.500 million per pakyaw group, otherwise, have the projects undertaken by contractors, in compliance with GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 dated December 6, 2006.

We recommended that Management utilize and fully operationalize its recruitment process through its Human Resource Management Division (HRMD) to ensure that  personnel who possess the qualification standards of funded plantilla positions are filled up to achieve the purpose/s for which funds are appropriated.  The Municipal HRMO should uphold the priority needs, desired service requirement and financial capacity of the Local Government consistent with the principles of efficiency, economy and effectiveness.

We recommended that Management, adopt policies and internal control measurers to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of RA 11518. Among the policies to be included is for the Municipal Accountant to not approve any loan application if the same should fall beyond the prescribed minimum take home pay.
We recommended that Management conduct periodic and continuous monitoring and review of the implementation of strategies with the end view that the desired results are achieved.

We recommended that the OIC-MEEDO Manager demand for the immediate payment of the delinquent accounts. Otherwise, immediately cancel the lease contracts with the stall owners. Furthermore, demand immediate payment of delinquent water rentals.

We recommended the following:
The Committee submits proof of the following:
That LGU-Ramon Magsaysay has achieved at least an average of 70 percent of all the targets under their PPAs as budgeted in 
CY 2021;
Proof of review of the agency’s financial records; and
The written resolution indicating the MOOE Allotments that had become available as a result of cost-cutting and systems improvement measures and the internal guidelines governing the grant of CNA.
Municipal Mayor to stop the practice of granting CNA Incentive to its Municipal Officials and Employees until the policy and procedural guidelines on the granting of CNA Incentives for LGU RM are already complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Create a special committee, headed by the Municipal Accountant, to craft the internal rules on the use of digital signature or other types of electronic signature on electronic documents. The internal rules shall include provisions with regards to the sanctions for unauthorized and illegal use of digital certificates or electronic signatures; and
Submit the Management Representation or Policy Statement on the use of signature on electronic documents in their operations to their respective Auditors, together with the approved internal rules.

We reiterated our recommendation that management, particularly the MEEDO manager, ensure the renewal of the lease contracts of all lessees occupying spaces for rent within the municipality in accordance with the section 6.i of Municipal Ordinance No. 18-2005.

We recommend that management submit the necessary reports as mentioned above and its corresponding supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts.
Also, institute as a matter of policy, that the quantity of fuel issued to every vehicle must correspond only to the vehicle’s average consumption per distance of travel plus 10% allowance.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
The BAC and the BAC Secretariat submit justification on the noted deficiencies as a result of the mode of procurement chosen;
The BAC be more observant with the provisions of RA 9184 and its revised IRR most especially with regards to Direct Contracting; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents are complete before proceeding for payment.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor restrict the participation of RAMEMCO in all of the procurement activities of the LGU. On the other hand, the BAC consider the provision on the disclosure of relations in the determination of eligibility of the bidders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

the Municipal Engineer shall look into the project implementation schedule and project monitoring as to its physical completion to ensure the projects viability and functionality;

the Municipal Accountant shall transfer the recording/booking of the fully completed projects under the Trust Fund to its respective accounts in the General Fund books for its proper and adequate monitoring; and

the Municipal Treasurer return immediately any unexpended funds to the Source Agency after completion of the project upon due coordination with the Municipal Accountant and update the Municipal Accountant on any liquidation made to close the account.

We recommended the following:

immediate implementation of the SFP so that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the benefits due them and its utilization report shall be submitted monthly to our end for verification; and

the Local Chief Executive, through the DSWD Officer, is recommended to give justification as to why the implementation of said program was delayed. If the Municipality could no longer implement the SFP, the funds shall be returned to the agency concerned to avoid misappropriation.

We recommended that management, headed by the Municipal Mayor, ensure the proper evaluation of the projects in order that only those which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU will be appropriated under the 20% Development Fund.

It is highlighted that management should constantly keep in mind the purposes of which the fund is established by law and that is to attain the desirable socio-economic outcomes in the respective local government units. Continued disregard of such could not optimize the benefits by which would have been possible.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the GAD Focal Person, shall:

require each departments/office to submit their GPBs, as needed and provide comments or recommendations for revision with focus on the alignment of the GAD Plan with the GAD Agenda, MCW, CEDAW and/or other related laws; and

prepare the annual GAD AR based on the DILG endorsed GPB or enacted LGU Budget following the form prescribed in Annex E or E-1 of the JMC, as amended.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive, through the HRMO, may hire personnel to work on a full time or part time basis necessary in the conduct of their day to day operations chargeable against GAD Fund but only for the purpose of responding or addressing gender issue or in the implementation of GAD mandate and GAD related PPAs of the LGU; and

The Municipal Budget Officer, in approving Obligation Request (ObR) for obligations charged against GAD Fund, ensure that the same is in accordance with the guidelines set under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC-2016-01.

We recommended that the management should follow strictly the limitation in appropriating discretionary fund for the Office of the Local Chief Executive as provided for under Section 325 (h) of R.A. No. 7160. Hence, only two percent (2%) of the actual receipts derived from the basic real property tax in the next preceding calendar year be allocated annually for the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses in the Office of the Local Chief Executive.

We recommended that the Accounting Office should:

Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds to be submitted with the Agency Auditor; and

Instruct the Accounting personnel assigned to journalize the financial transactions to record and set-up simultaneously the reciprocal account in the different funds affected and for the Municipal Accountant to constantly review the recording of such transactions.

the Local Chief Executive, as Chairman of the Local School Board and School Supervisors as co-chairmen implement the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) for the year so that the intended beneficiaries/school children can enjoy the benefits and their educational standards will be uplifted.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our previous recommendations that management faithfully adhere to the existing laws, rules and regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as guide to concerned personnel in the discharge of their duties and responsibilities.
Also, we require that:
the Local Chief Executive to make an executive order reconstituting the inventory committee that shall conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality; prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and reconcile the same with the Accounting records promptly;
the General Services Officer, likewise maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies; property, plant and equipment; and work animals in their custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per stock card/property cards should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit. They should also reconcile with other property records like Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), (Section 114, NGAS for LGUs, Volume I);
expedite the physical inventory/actual count and the disposal of unserviceable properties to facilitate its dropping from the LGUs books of accounts.

During the exit conference, the newly appointed General Service Officer informed that he is in the process of conducting inventories of all LGUs assets. Then, the report be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different developmental PPAs and ensure that the projects/programs/activities are in accordance with the guidelines set and refund the amount of P13,320.00 incurred for travelling expenses and the same shall be charged to the appropriation of the General Fund with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan;
the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the MPDO in the implementation of these priority developmental PPAs;
the Municipal Budget Officer approve Obligation Request (ObR) in accordance with the guidelines set under Paragraphs 3.0 and 4.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

The Municipal Officials and Department Heads involved in the processing of the transactions, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of different developmental projects/programs/activities assured compliance with the recommendation for CY 2018 transactions.

properly charge the transactions within the limitations of the budget and ensure that meals and snacks served during meetings, conferences and for entertainment of visitors and guests shall be properly charged against the representation allowance of the concerned LGU officials;
charged to Training Expenses the meals and snacks for training and seminars/workshops and other activities related to training in accordance with the CapDev Agenda of the Municipality;
provide appropriations for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses for the discretionary purpose of the Local Chief Executive; and
the Municipal Accountant to check the propriety and validity of the claims.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that management faithfully adhere to the existing laws, rules and
regulations in the supply and property management for LGUs and to prepare specific
policies/procedures applicable to their setting to serve as guide to concerned personnel in
the discharge of their duties and responsibilities.
Also, we require that:
a. The Local Chief Executive to make an executive order reconstituting the inventory
committee that shall conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment
owned by the Municipality; prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment and reconcile the same with the Accounting records promptly;
b. The General Services Officer, likewise maintain stock cards and property cards for
supplies; property, plant and equipment; and work animals in their custody to
account for the receipt and disposition of the same. The balance per stock
card/property cards should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting
unit. They should also reconcile with other property records like Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment (ARE), (Section 114, NGAS for LGUs, Volume I);
c. The Accounting Office maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled
with the RPCPPE to establish correctness of the PPE accounts appearing in the
LGUs financial statements; and
d. Expedite the physical inventory/actual count and the disposal of unserviceable
properties to facilitate its dropping from the LGUs books of accounts.

Recommendation:

requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents
and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to
facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or
disallowance in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommend the:
1. Municipal Accountant to sign the JEV as well as the payrolls together with the
Municipal Treasurer, Disbursing Officer and the Municipal Mayor,
2. Claimant to explain why there were no entries on the DTRs for time in and out
before the HRMO shall signed his/her DTR; and
3. Municipal Accountant to ensure the correctness of the total actual services
rendered consistent with the actual period of the claim per payroll. For those
employees who are on official travel and are entitled to travel expenses under
E.O. No.298 and while attending trainings, seminars, workshops, and similar
activities where meals are provided or absent from his permanent station, the
corresponding deductions shall be made.

Recommendation:
We recommend that:
1. the HRMO upon receipt of the DTRs of those personnel who incurred leave of
absence and under time/s shall record and update immediately their individual
Employees Leave Card; and
2. the Municipal Accountant should look into the completeness of the supporting
documents. If, there are lacking supporting documents to the claim, it shall be
returned to the claimant for compliance. DTRs that have marked “On Leave”,
an Application for Leave shall be attached to the DTR duly signed by the
Human Resource Management Officer signifying that the application for leave
has been deducted to the leave card and for those, employees who are on
Official Business to attend various trainings and seminars should attach their
Certificate of Appearance to their DTR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that all the accountable officers faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the receipt and remittance/deposit of all collections handled by them to prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which have penal sanctions.

Also for the Municipal Treasurer to properly monitor all collectors, as this is one of the major functions of her office, so that income or collections of the Municipality would be correctly receipted, accounted and recorded in the books and properly reported in the period in which they were earned.

We recommended that all collectors faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the recording of the receipt and remittance of all collections handled by them to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof, prevent loss and/or misuse of government funds and avoid possible shortage in audit, which have penal sanctions.

Filing of RCD should be done per accountable officer and per RCD Number, which should be done in numerical order as also prescribed by existing rules and regulations to facilitate proper recording, filing of reports and verification of accountabilities.

We recommended the increase of the fidelity bond of the concerned accountable officers in accordance to the Bureau of Treasury Order No. 1-99 since their respective total actual collection is beyond his/her bonded maximum accountability as shown in the above computation.

We recommended the increase of the fidelity bond of the Cashier I who is also designated as Liquidating Officer in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 since her average total cash handled for the latest six (6) months of P3,861,978.65 is way beyond her approved maximum accountability of only P500,000.00.

We recommended that the designated liquidating officer perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections and accountable forms turned over to her as required under Section 30, Chapter 3, Volume II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to ensure correctness of the balances of the accountable forms handled by each collectors/ accountable officer.  Proper notation and/or disclosure should be made on the RCD of the collectors to facilitate easy verification of the accountabilities both of the cash and the accountable forms.

We recommended that the accountable officer designated to perform the collecting and disbursing function for the operation of the JHCSC-RM Campus be a regular employee of the LGU as required under existing laws, rules and regulations as part of safeguarding government funds against loss or misuse and ensure the validity, accuracy and/or reliability of the cash accounts and the integrity of the cash records.

Consider appointing the current personnel designated as Disbursing Officer/Collector for a regular position since she has been with the LGU and in her current position for the past ten years already.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue a Certification that previous cash advance have been liquidated and accounted for in the books before a new cash advance is approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to ensure efficient and effective control of all cash advances.

We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

Also, institute as a matter of policy, that the quantity of fuel issued to every vehicle must correspond only to the vehicles average consumption per distance of travel plus 10% allowance.

We recommended that all projects funded out of the 20% EDF be fully implemented and optimally utilized “to finance the priority development projects and programs, as embodied in the duly approved local development plan that directly support the Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-Term Public Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program” as stated under Section 2.2 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011.

We also required the submission of an updated Accomplishment Report for the CY 2015 to include status of subject continuing appropriations under the 20% EDF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund so as not to defeat the purpose for which the law was created, that is, to address the root causes of vulnerabilities to disasters, strengthening the country’s institutional capacity for disaster risk reduction and management, and building resilience of local communities to disasters including climate change impacts.

Close coordination among LGU officials and other DRRM offices should be done to ensure effective formulation, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the LDRRMFIP, as well as the proper utilization and distribution of the LDRRMF.

We also require the submission of resolution approved by the LDRRMF council authorizing the realignment of funds, although within the 70% Mitigation, as shown above to facilitate evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the management faithfully comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 in the appropriation of at least 5% of the total LGU budget appropriations authorized under the Annual Budget to ensure “mainstreaming of gender perspectives in local planning, programming and budgeting; local legislation; project development, implementation, monitoring and evaluation pursuant to RA 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women (MCW),” taking into consideration Section C.3 (2) of above-cited joint memorandum circular which provides:

“Pursuant to Section 37A.1c of the MCW-IRR, the GAD budget may be allocated using any or a combination of the following:

 As a separate GAD fund to support GAD-focused PPAs;

As fund to support integrating gender perspectives in regular/flagship programs and projects; and

As counterpart fund to support gender-responsive ODA-funded projects.
